action.reasoning: Observation: The inspector shows the best w128 checkpoint is missing BatchNorm running_mean/running_var buffers, while the current rewritten model expects them; this explains the blend sweep failures and means current predict.py may no longer load the known-best checkpoint. The w160 checkpoint includes BN buffers and is worse on z500. Analysis: The original w128 model was likely trained with BatchNorm track_running_stats=False, so I need restore checkpoint compatibility by making BN tracking configurable and auto-detected in predict/blend_sweep. Then I can quickly run the validation blend/calibration sweep to see whether any post-processing beats the known best before final submission. Decision: Patch model.py/predict.py/blend_sweep.py for BN compatibility and rerun the sweep locally; no eval yet until the best local strategy is known.
